title: Mind Your Step (by Step): Chain-of-Thought can Reduce Performance on Tasks where Thinking Makes Humans Worse

methodology.approach: Drawing a parallel between CoT prompting and humans engaging in verbal thought
methodology.heuristic: Tasks where thinking decreases human performance might also be tasks where CoT harms model performance

tasks_evaluated.name: Implicit Statistical Learning (ISL)
tasks_evaluated.description: Classification of strings generated by an artificial grammar

tasks_evaluated.name: Face Recognition (FR)
tasks_evaluated.description: Recognition of a face from a set that shares similar descriptions

tasks_evaluated.name: Classification of Data with Exceptions (CDE)
tasks_evaluated.description: Learning labels in the presence of exceptions

findings.human_performance: Humans show reductions in performance when engaging in verbal thinking in all tasks

model_performance.first_three: The first three tasks (ISL, FR, CDE) have similar effects on LLMs and VLMs
model_performance.last_three: The last three tasks (NLI, SI, WM) differ between humans and models in meaningful ways

results.main_goal: To identify and predict when CoT has negative effects on model performance
results.key_insight: Despite differences between models and humans, some failure cases are related to shared traits between humans and models
results.anticipated_finding: CoT will hurt model performance in cases where deliberation hurts performance in humans
